title: [lichen_l2.c:328-378] peer_try_all_pubkeys saves/restores ctx but out_len restoration is redundant
description:
Category not checked: Code complexity hiding bugs

Bug found: In peer_try_all_pubkeys(), lines 335-337 save three values:
  size_t saved_out_len = *out_len;
  const uint8_t *saved_peer_pubkey = ctx->peer_pubkey;
  const uint8_t *saved_peer_eui64 = ctx->peer_eui64;

On success (line 351), we return immediately without restoring anything - correct, we want to keep the ctx modifications.

On auth failure continuing loop (implicit after line 365), we restore *out_len at line 347 but NOT peer_pubkey/peer_eui64. These get overwritten at lines 345-346 on next iteration anyway.

On non-auth error (lines 366-370), we restore all three values. But wait - why?
- ctx->peer_pubkey and ctx->peer_eui64 were NULL on entry (per line 1156-1157)
- We restore them to NULL (saved values)
- Then caller receives the error and doesn't use ctx

This is harmless defensive coding, but the asymmetry between success/non-auth-error paths suggests incomplete reasoning about invariants.

Impact: P4 - Code complexity, no functional bug.
close_reason: Fixed: Added clarifying comment explaining that ctx restoration on error paths maintains the invariant that ctx is unmodified on failure, even though caller doesn't use ctx on error.
